title: Lung Cancer Screening with LDCT - Coverage Criteria

policy.description: Medicare covers screening for lung cancer with LDCT once per year when all criteria are met

span.text: Medicare covers screening for lung cancer with low dose computed tomography (LDCT) once per year when all of the following criteria are met:
The beneficiary must be Age 50 - 77 years.
The beneficiary must have a tobacco smoking history of at least 20 pack-years.

children.description: Current smoker or quit within last 15 years

span.text: The beneficiary must be a current smoker or one who has quit smoking within the last 15 years.
The beneficiary must be asymptomatic (no signs or symptoms of lung cancer).

children.description: Written order for LDCT during counseling/SDM visit (HCPCS G0296) prior to first screening

span.text: The beneficiary must receive a written order for LDCT lung cancer screening during a lung cancer screening counseling and shared decision-making visit (HCPCS G0296) prior to the first screening.
Covered procedure codes include HCPCS G0296 (counseling/SDM visit) and CPT 71271 (CT thorax low dose for lung cancer screening).
